Participates in online maker community forums daily but never joins video chats; prefers written communication
Runs social media ads only for new product launches — never for ongoing items
Never snacks during late evening work sessions; sticks to herbal tea only
Plans quarterly family day trips to nearby towns — never goes on overnight vacations
Preps breakfast for the family every morning before anyone wakes — never skips this even on weekends
Checks Etsy shop stats immediately after preschool drop-off — never during the afternoon
No screens allowed at the dinner table — enforces this rule strictly with the entire family
Reviews weekly calendar every Sunday evening with spouse — never plans last-minute activities
Does not engage in any carving work at all due to therapist's orders; focuses only on finishing and packaging existing items during allowed work sessions
No personal woodworking/carving projects at all; pursues non-physical creative hobbies after bedtime while injury persists
Performs daily rehabilitation exercises for lower back pain each morning as prescribed, without exceptions until cleared by the therapist

No longer reads late at night; chooses short, calming activities (e.g., brief mindfulness meditation or gentle stretching) before the strictly scheduled bedtime to improve sleep quality per CPAP regimen
Follows a strict, physician-mandated bedtime each night to support CPAP use — avoids all stimulating activities after 9pm
Performs a brief wind-down routine, including setting up the CPAP device, dimming lights, and 5 minutes of guided breathing before bed
Structures weekday routines independently of spouse’s work hours, since family dinners now happen without her on most weekdays due to standard day shifts
Prepares and eats weekday family dinners without the spouse; switches to sending a family mealtime recap message and photo after the children are in bed
Replaces after-lunch walks with seated mindfulness meditation for mental clarity — avoids all weight-bearing activities while recovering from ankle sprain
Pauses all family weekend hikes; prioritizes indoor family activities until cleared for hiking post-sprain
Refreshes kids’ play routines with more storytime and tabletop games to minimize active movement during his ankle recovery

Prioritizes responding to Etsy customer messages from new buyers within 4 hours, but allows regular inquiries and messages from returning customers to wait up to 24 hours due to high volume after going viral
Shifts from handwritten thank-you notes to pre-printed cards for packaging most orders, reserving handwritten notes only for special/custom requests during periods of heavy order volume
Now checks Etsy shop stats multiple times throughout the day, including afternoons and evenings, to monitor ongoing order surges and troubleshoot issues in real time
Switches to texting his parents weekly to stay in touch, but no longer organizes in-person childcare visits due to their relocation out of state
Utilizes evening screen time (kids’ educational shows) 1-2 times per week when solo parenting, to free up time for dinner preparation or work catch-up, despite previous reluctance
Prepares vegetarian, high-fiber, low-saturated-fat dinners for the whole family nightly — proactively avoids processed snacks and fatty dairy products in all recipes
Introduces daily family fruit bowls as a main after-dinner snack, fully eliminating chips, cookies, or processed treats for everyone
Now runs errands on Saturdays after the artisan market concludes, shifting away from exclusive Tuesday mornings due to booth obligations and increased weekend activities.
Enjoys interacting with regular artisan market customers in person every Saturday, making friendly conversation and giving brief live carving demonstrations when permitted.

Now prioritizes building wholesale relationships and collaborates with local boutiques and galleries on exclusive Timber Threads collections, shifting focus away from regular solo artisan market vending
Holds monthly in-person meetings with local boutique owners or gallery curators to plan Timber Threads product lines and joint promotions
Reserves weekday early afternoon sessions for assembling larger batch orders and preparing exclusive packaging for wholesale clients

Actively avoids scheduling playdates and group activities for his preschool-aged daughter, instead prioritizing low-key one-on-one time at home or with family while she copes with her close friend's departure
Introduces a daily after-preschool quiet ritual (such as drawing or reading together) to provide extra reassurance and connection for his daughter during this emotionally difficult transition

superseded_preferences.fact: Responds to Etsy customer messages within 2 hours during working hours — avoids letting replies linger overnight
superseded_preferences.reason: The sudden surge in orders and new customer messages from the influencer’s post makes it infeasible to reply to every inquiry within 2 hours. He now actively triages, responding rapidly only to new/influencer-generated contacts and letting low-priority replies wait until workload permits.

superseded_preferences.fact: Completes all carving work in morning sessions — leaves finishing and packaging for evenings
superseded_preferences.reason: Severe lower back pain has led the physical therapist to recommend a complete pause on carving activities. Continuing carving work, in any session, would contradict medical advice and worsen his condition.

superseded_preferences.fact: Does back stretches every morning before starting work — never skips even on busy days
superseded_preferences.reason: The prescribed rehabilitation plan replaces regular morning stretches with a new routine focused on recovery exercises, making the old stretching regimen irrelevant during this period.

superseded_preferences.fact: Cooks vegetarian dinners for the family every night — never makes separate meals for himself
superseded_preferences.reason: His doctor has advised urgent dietary changes due to elevated cholesterol, including increased fiber and reduced saturated fats and processed snacks. He must now consciously modify family recipes to exclude high-fat cheeses, butter, cream, and processed vegetarian snacks, changing how he cooks.

superseded_preferences.fact: Goes for a short walk after lunch for mental clarity — never walks in the evening
superseded_preferences.reason: Due to doctor’s instructions to avoid weight-bearing activities, he can no longer take his daily walk after lunch, a previously essential mental clarity ritual. Seated mindfulness now replaces walking.

superseded_preferences.fact: Prioritizes family weekend hikes over solo exercise — only rarely works out alone
superseded_preferences.reason: His moderate ankle sprain requires avoidance of any hiking or weight-bearing activity for at least six weeks. The family hiking tradition must temporarily stop and switch to alternatives.

superseded_preferences.fact: Schedules playdates with other stay-at-home parents at least once a week — never leaves social plans spontaneous
superseded_preferences.reason: The ability to pre-schedule weekly playdates relied heavily on his parents' regular help; without their childcare support, his week-to-week availability becomes less predictable, making this strict, advance planning impractical.

superseded_preferences.fact: Calls his parents every Monday to organize the week’s childcare visit — avoids texting for family coordination
superseded_preferences.reason: With his parents moving away and no longer available for regular in-person childcare, coordinating weekly visits is no longer relevant. He now maintains connection by texting, as calling is less necessary for logistics.

superseded_preferences.fact: Always organizes weekday routines around spouse’s shift schedule; never overlaps dinner time with late shifts
superseded_preferences.reason: His spouse’s new permanent day-shift job means she is away during standard dinner time; the previous coordination to avoid dinner overlap with late shifts no longer applies. He must organize routines not around her shift changes, but around her new predictable absence during dinner.

superseded_preferences.fact: Hosts monthly craft-making sessions for neighborhood families — never attends craft fairs solo
superseded_preferences.reason: The new booth at the local artisan market requires him to represent Timber Threads on his own every Saturday, directly contradicting his previous habit of never attending craft fairs solo. This move to in-person, solo participation changes his social approach.

superseded_preferences.fact: Sends photo updates of the kids to his spouse daily after preschool pickup, never misses a weekday
superseded_preferences.reason: With the spouse now unavailable during weekday afternoons due to standard hospital hours, sending photo updates after pickup is less meaningful, as she cannot check them at work. This routine loses its relevance with her daytime absence.

superseded_preferences.fact: Works on personal woodworking projects only after the kids’ bedtime — never carves for fun during the day
superseded_preferences.reason: A temporary halt on all carving includes leisure activities. He cannot pursue woodworking projects, even for fun, and must seek alternative hobbies.

superseded_preferences.fact: Explores new local hiking trails with family every Sunday — never repeats the same trail twice in a month
superseded_preferences.reason: The ankle injury removes the circumstance of Sunday family hikes entirely, as hiking is impossible during recovery; there’s no replacement activity for this specific habit.

superseded_preferences.fact: Bakes bread from scratch on Saturday mornings — never buys bakery bread for the family
superseded_preferences.reason: This preference likely relied on baking recipes using ingredients that are often high in saturated fat (e.g., butter, whole milk, eggs) which the doctor now forbids. Until he can adapt his bread recipes or sources, the circumstance for this baking ritual doesn't exist.

superseded_preferences.fact: Reads paper books on woodworking techniques late at night — never uses digital guides or ebooks
superseded_preferences.reason: To adhere to the prescribed strict sleep schedule for effective CPAP therapy and better long-term health, he must avoid stimulating activities like reading woodworking books late into the night and instead focus on calming activities that support an early, consistent bedtime.

superseded_preferences.fact: Schedules weekly errands for Tuesday mornings only — never runs errands on weekends
superseded_preferences.reason: Securing a Saturday booth means Tuesday mornings are no longer ideal for all weekly errands, and he needs to address supply runs or business errands after market participation. He can no longer avoid weekend errands.

superseded_preferences.fact: Engages in light, non-crafting creative activities (like sketching or digital design) after the kids’ bedtime to maintain mental wellbeing during the carving pause
superseded_preferences.reason: With a mandatory strictly scheduled bedtime to address sleep apnea, he can no longer spend discretionary time on evening creative activities after the kids sleep; the prior routine fails to fit the new medical requirements.

superseded_preferences.fact: Organizes weekend indoor craft sessions for the family in place of hiking, using child-friendly projects that don't require standing
superseded_preferences.reason: His new Saturday morning booth commitment as a market vendor eliminates the opportunity to host indoor craft sessions for the family during weekends; the event replaces the previous routine and the underlying circumstance is gone.

superseded_preferences.fact: Now relies on last-minute, flexible playdates with other local parents or accepts spontaneous visits, due to increased solo parenting and unpredictable childcare gaps after his parents' move
superseded_preferences.reason: His daughter's increased reluctance and withdrawal following her best friend's move lead him to intentionally pause social playdate organizing, focusing on gentle support and minimizing potentially stressful group interactions.
